differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Enforces schema with predefined tables, columns, and data types for consistency.
differences.rightDesc: Schemaless by default, allowing dynamic structures and nested documents.

differences.title: 2. SCHEMA
differences.leftDesc: Requires defining schema before data insertion, ensuring data integrity.
differences.rightDesc: Schema is inferred or enforced at the application level, offering flexibility.

differences.title: 3. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: Powerful and standardized Structured Query Language for complex data retrieval.
differences.rightDesc: Uses a rich query language based on JSON documents for flexible searching.

differences.title: 4. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Primarily scales vertically by increasing server resources (CPU, RAM).
differences.rightDesc: Designed for horizontal scaling by adding more servers (sharding).

differences.title: 5. TRANSACTIONS
differences.leftDesc: Guarantees Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability for reliable transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Prioritizes availability and partition tolerance, transactions may take time to reflect.

differences.title: 6. RELATIONSHIPS
differences.leftDesc: Handles relationships between tables using foreign keys and JOIN operations.
differences.rightDesc: Stores related data within a single document, simplifying reads for related items.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for complex analytical queries, financial systems, and data warehousing.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for rapidly evolving applications, catalogs, and real-time analytics.
